evidence: No mutable static fields exist anywhere in the codebase: every static field is either `private static final Logger` or a `private static final` constant (e.g. `DEFAULT_CORS_ALLOW_ORIGIN`, `RELATION_ALIASES`).
No static initializer blocks (`static { ... }`) were found in any class.

`System.getProperty("cors.allow.origin", ...)` is read inside `HttpApiUtils.sendOptionsResponse()` at call time, not in a static field initializer — meaning it reflects the current JVM state on each call and does not create a stale-snapshot hazard.
`System.getProperty("grammar.config", ...)` in `GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish()` and in `Main.handleBlackLabServerCommand()` is read at method-invocation time, not at class-load time.

`BlackLabQueryExecutor` opens the BlackLab index in its constructor; this is intentional and documented with a Javadoc note explaining why constructor-level I/O is appropriate for this lifecycle model.
`WordSketchApiServer` explicitly separates construction from port binding: the Javadoc comment `// NOTE: Port binding is deferred to start()` confirms the design.
All dependencies (`QueryExecutor`, `GrammarConfig`, `SemanticFieldExplorer`) flow through constructors — no class reaches out to a global registry or ambient context.
`GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish()` is a static factory method with documented two-tier exception handling; it is not wired into any static field initializer or class-load hook.
initialization_coupling.issues_preventing_higher_score: The `createDefaultEnglish()` static factory method hides file I/O and a `System.getProperty` read behind a static call surface. While it is never invoked at class-load time, a future developer who wires it into a static field or DI container annotation could silently introduce an import-time side effect. The method exists alongside the explicit `load(Path)` API that is already used in `Main`, creating a dual-path access pattern where the more dangerous convenience path may be reached for in test helpers or future DI scaffolding.

strengths: No mutable static fields anywhere: every class-level static is `final`, making class-load state fully deterministic and safe across test runs.

System.getProperty reads are consistently deferred to method-invocation time (`sendOptionsResponse`, `createDefaultEnglish`, `handleBlackLabServerCommand`) rather than captured in static field initializers, eliminating stale-snapshot risk.
All cross-component dependencies are injected via constructors (`QueryExecutor`, `GrammarConfig`, `SemanticFieldExplorer`), with no ambient singletons or global registries.
Port binding in `WordSketchApiServer` is explicitly separated from construction, with a comment marking the deferral as intentional — showing active awareness of initialization-order concerns.
Constructor-level I/O in `BlackLabQueryExecutor` is justified and documented in the Javadoc, rather than hidden.
initialization_coupling.issue_character: No concrete initialization-coupling defects were found; the only flag is a latent risk: the `createDefaultEnglish()` static factory method provides a convenience path that reads `System.getProperty` and performs file I/O, which could be misused as a static field initializer in future code.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: By the dimension rubric (boot-order dependencies, import-time side effects, global singletons with ordering constraints), this codebase has none of the listed defects: no class-level I/O, no mutable globals, no implicit ordering between class loads. This places the codebase firmly in the 'exemplary' range. A small deduction from 100 is warranted because `createDefaultEnglish()` is an untested latent risk — it is a static method whose signature does not convey that it performs I/O and reads environment state, which violates the 'interface honesty' principle for initialization code and could become a real coupling issue if future code wires it into a static field or DI lifecycle annotation.

possible_false_positives: mutable_globals scan signal — mechanical scan found no mutable globals; confirmed by code inspection
